spec_match.rationale: Deduplication via sharedStorage.get/set with a 'seen_<campaign>' key plus interestGroups() membership check is the canonical pattern in the Turtledove reach-measurement explainer; the registered worklet uses contributeToHistogram with a fixed bucketOffset which is the spec-correct shape.

interactive.rationale: Real campaign select + Run worklet button that attempts sharedStorage.worklet.addModule + sharedStorage.run, plus a synthetic user grid so the deduplication semantics are visible without flags.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises a worklet simulation over a mock user population — the page does show the user grid, but the 'simulation' never actually walks the user list and shows which users would have been double-counted vs deduplicated; the table#resultTable element is declared but never populated by the JS.

edge_cases.rationale: The 'Already counted?' / 'Action' columns are declared in the table head but no row is ever appended — the deduplication outcome is never rendered. The simulated walk over USERS is missing entirely; only the live worklet path runs.

references.rationale: see-also section is absent. No chromestatus link, no Turtledove reach-measurement link — violates invariant #3.

design_system.rationale: Variables used throughout, but the accent-coloured IG pills (accent-blue, accent-amber background with white text via the global pill rule? Actually they have no explicit colour) are likely to fail WCAG AA contrast on the amber background; needs an audit.

openQuestions.title: Wire the user-by-user dedup walk into the result table
openQuestions.detail: The page declares a #resultTable with columns User / Matching IGs / Already counted? / Action, but nothing populates it. Implement a synthetic walk over USERS that (a) computes which IGs match the campaign prefix, (b) marks the first match as counted and subsequent campaigns for the same user as deduped, (c) renders a row per user with the colour-coded outcome. That is the entire reason the demo exists — without it the page is just a code listing.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus + Turtledove references
openQuestions.detail: There is no see-also section at all. Add chromestatus.com/feature/5074536530444288 (invariant #3) and the WICG Turtledove PA_reach.md explainer link to satisfy the references rubric.

openQuestions.title: Audit IG-pill contrast
openQuestions.detail: The .ig-pill.sport / .ig-pill.travel etc. set background to accent-blue/amber/rose without setting a foreground colour, so they inherit the page's --text-black on a coloured background. Some combinations may pass (blue + black) but amber + black likely fails AA for the 0.72rem font size; check each pill and either set colour explicitly per pill or switch to a higher-contrast palette.

openQuestions.title: Show the 'overcounting without dedup' baseline
openQuestions.detail: The whole point of the feature is to avoid double-counting; the demo should let visitors flip a 'dedup off' switch and watch the histogram count rise from N to N+repeats, making the win visible. Without that contrast the dedup behaviour is invisible.

summary: Card promises a worklet that walks a mock user population and demonstrates deduplication; the page sets up the grid and the controls but never wires the result table, so the actual dedup outcome is invisible. Add the walk, add references, audit pill contrast. The most fixable critique in the feature.
